title: Brent oil jumps 3%+ to $99.58 on Iran retaliation fears — WTI $93.89, reverses 5/26 lows
snippet: Brent crude futures gained more than 3% to close at $99.58/bbl on May 26, reversing the previous week's decline to $96.31. The jump was driven by Iran's retaliation threats after US self-defense strikes and the IRGC MQ-9 shootdown claim. WTI futures settled at $93.89/bbl (-3% vs Friday due to Memorial Day holiday dislocation). Trump said talks with Tehran were 'progressing well' but warned additional attacks could follow if negotiations broke down.
tag_reason: Significant oil price reversal — Brent rebounds 3%+ from $96 to $99 on retaliation fears
